summary: Comprehensive backend regression suite (27 pytest cases) executed against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. 26/27 PASS. Discovered one real defect (silent error-swallowing in aimmh.fan_out / daisy_chain) and a related curated-inventory mismatch where several emergent model_ids are not in the Emergent key's allowed-model list (notably 'anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6' and 'openai:gpt-5.4').

critical.issue: Silent failure: a provider error returned via emergent_provider.chat() is dropped by interdependent_lib/aimmh/patterns.py::_invoke when wrapping the result dict into ModelResult. The function reads only content/usage/meta and never copies the 'error' key, so the API responds 200 with content='' and error=None. Reproduced 3/3 times with model_ids=['emergent:openai:gpt-4o-mini','emergent:anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6']: the second model returns empty content and null error. Calling /api/chat/single with the same model surfaces the real underlying litellm AuthenticationError ('key not allowed to access model … Tried to access claude-sonnet-4-6').

Several curated model IDs are NOT in the Emergent LLM key's allowed list and therefore 100% fail at chat time: 'openai:gpt-5.4', 'openai:gpt-5.4-mini', 'anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6'. Allowed substitutes (from litellm error payload) are e.g. 'gpt-5.1' / 'gpt-5' / 'gpt-5-mini' for OpenAI and 'claude-sonnet-4-5' / 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929' for Anthropic. The starter agents 'research-council' and 'daisy-prover' default to these invalid IDs, so the out-of-the-box agents silently produce empty responses (because of bug #1 above).

minor.issue: intent param is accepted in body via embed=True; works fine, but undocumented. Consider making the request model a Pydantic class for clarity.

Body re-uses SessionUpsert which makes ALL fields optional via the `if v is not None` filter except updated_at — fine, but persona='' or selected_models=[] cannot be cleared because the filter strips falsy-None. Minor UX nit.

action_items: FIX (root cause) interdependent_lib/aimmh/patterns.py::_invoke — propagate the 'error' field: ModelResult(..., error=r.get('error')). Without this, every fan_out/daisy_chain/council call swallows provider errors and returns empty content.

FIX providers/emergent_provider.py::_INVENTORY — replace invalid IDs ('gpt-5.4', 'gpt-5.4-mini', 'claude-sonnet-4-6') with allowed Emergent models (e.g. 'gpt-5.1' / 'gpt-5-mini', 'claude-sonnet-4-5' / 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929'). Also reconcile starter agent defaults in server.py::_on_startup (research-council, daisy-prover, zfae-classic) to use only valid IDs.
Optionally tighten emergent_provider.chat() to treat empty SDK responses as an explicit error so the dict result always carries a non-null 'error' on failure.
critical_code_review_comments: ChatResult is a TypedDict (= plain dict at runtime); _call_model relies on **result spread. This works but is fragile — consider a Pydantic model so missing fields are explicit.
server.py is 715 lines and mixes routing, persistence, and AIMMH plumbing. Split routers (keys, vault, sessions, drafts, chat, inspector, agents, usage) into separate modules for maintainability.

emergent_provider.session_id is built from str(abs(hash((real, len(messages)))))[:10] — Python hash() is randomized per process and message-len-dependent, which makes the 'short-cache' contract opaque. Use a deterministic hash (e.g. md5) if cache semantics matter, or drop the caching note from the docstring.

usage records use {'total': 0} when routed via Emergent (Emergent SDK doesn't expose usage). This is expected per the review note; consider surfacing 'usage_unknown=true' so the UI can distinguish 'zero tokens' from 'unmeasured'.
/api/inspector/heartbeat uses embed=True for a single optional field — acceptable but a Pydantic body model is cleaner.
All endpoints use user_id='local' default — fine for single-user mode, but there is no auth boundary. Document this as an MVP constraint.
test_credentials: n/a — single-user mode with user_id='local'. EMERGENT_LLM_KEY already in backend/.env. No login flow.
seed_data_creation: Tests create then delete all artifacts (TEST_-prefixed keys, vault entries, sessions, drafts, and the 'prompt-synthesis-hub' agent). Usage records are append-only and intentionally left in place.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run pytest after the main agent applies the _invoke() error-propagation fix and the inventory ID cleanup. Expected outcome: TestChat::test_chat_fanout passes (both models return content OR a real error). The single, daisychain (structural), and synthesize tests already pass. The pytest file is the regression source of truth — feel free to extend with: (a) explicit assertions that daisychain step content is non-empty for at least the first valid model, (b) starter-agent end-to-end smoke (load research-council → fanout against its default_models → expect content), (c) BYOK inventory error-map surface when an invalid api_key is stored. xAI remains intentionally untested (no user key).

fanout_empty_content_no_error.reproduction: POST /api/chat/fanout with model_ids=['emergent:openai:gpt-4o-mini','emergent:anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6'] → results[1] = {content:'', error:null}. POST /api/chat/single with model_id='emergent:anthropic:claude-sonnet-4-6' → result.error='emergent: Failed to generate chat completion: litellm.AuthenticationError: … key not allowed to access model … Tried to access claude-sonnet-4-6'.
fanout_empty_content_no_error.root_cause: Two layered defects. (1) Inventory contains a model name ('claude-sonnet-4-6') that the Emergent LLM key cannot access (allowed list does not include 4-6; nearest valid is claude-sonnet-4-5 / claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929). (2) interdependent_lib/aimmh/patterns.py::_invoke maps a dict result into ModelResult by copying ONLY content, usage, and meta — never error. Therefore the provider's error string is dropped, the API returns 200 with empty content and null error, and the bug is invisible to clients.
fanout_empty_content_no_error.mitigation: Patch _invoke to add `error=r.get('error')` (and optionally fold a non-empty error into ModelResult.error even when content is empty). Independently, correct the curated inventory and starter-agent defaults to use models actually in the Emergent allow-list.
